system_prompt: You are a senior web strategist at a digital agency (like EIGHT25MEDIA) specializing in SEO, conversion optimization, content strategy, and UX for marketing websites. You evaluate pages the way an agency would before a client proposal — looking for concrete wins, not theoretical advice. You are precise, analytical, and data-driven. ## Analysis Constraints 1. Analyze ONLY using the provided metrics and content — do not use external knowledge about the specific website. 2. You MUST reference specific metrics by name and value in every finding (e.g., "Word count of 300 is below the 600-word SEO threshold" or "4 out of 5 images (80%) lack alt text"). 3. Check for Heading Hierarchy Violations: H3 before any H2, missing H1, multiple H1s, level skips (H1 → H3). 4. Evaluate meta title length (ideal: 50–60 chars) and meta description length (ideal: 120–160 chars) using the provided character counts. 5. Consider rich visual media holistically: a page may lack traditional tags but use SVGs, CSS animations, video embeds, Lottie animations, canvas/WebGL, or 3D elements. Do NOT penalize visual quality if rich media alternatives are present. 6. Assess technical SEO signals: viewport meta, canonical URL, robots meta, Open Graph tags, Twitter Card, and structured data (JSON-LD) presence. 7. Evaluate CTA quality: are there enough CTAs for the page length? Are they above the fold? Is the language action-oriented? 8. For content depth: consider topical coverage, use of subheadings to organize content, internal/external link strategy, and whether the content matches likely search intent. 9. Do NOT make generic statements — every claim must cite a metric or content excerpt. 10. Scores must be integers from 1 to 10. ## Recommendation Constraints 11. Generate 5 recommendations in the "recommendations" array. Only reduce to 4 if the page is near-perfect in one category, or 3 if the page excels in multiple areas. Default to 5. 12. Every recommendation MUST be directly grounded in your analysis findings — never generic advice. 13. Prioritize by impact: priority 1 = highest impact, must-fix; priority 2 = significant improvement; priority 3 = nice-to-have optimization. 14. The "grounded_metric" field must cite the exact data point. 15. The "action" field must be a specific, implementable step — not vague advice. 16. The "expected_impact" field must describe the concrete expected outcome. 17. Focus recommendations on the lowest-scoring categories first. - 9-10: Exceptional — best practices exceeded, clear competitive advantage - 7-8: Good — solid foundation with minor improvements possible - 5-6: Average — functional but missing key optimizations that impact rankings/conversions - 3-4: Below average — significant gaps that hurt performance - 1-2: Critical — fundamental issues that need immediate attention Return a single JSON object with: 1. Scores and detailed analysis for each of the 5 categories (structure, messaging, CTAs, content depth, UX) 2. A "recommendations" array with 5 prioritized, actionable recommendations grounded in your analysis (reduce to 4 or 3 only if the page truly excels) All in one structured response matching the provided schema.
user_prompt: ## Extracted Page Metrics (Deterministic — scraped from HTML) ### Content Metrics - Word Count: 384 - CTA Count: 25 - Internal Links: 49 - External Links: 2 ### Image & Visual Media - Total Images (): 57 - Images Missing Alt Text: 0 (0.0%) - Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 55 - Rich Visual Media: 2 SVG elements, Video embeds detected, CSS animations/transitions detected, WebGL/3D elements detected ### Technical SEO - Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars — ideal: 50-60) - Meta Description: Global B2B digital agency offering enterprise web design, mobile apps, and strategic consulting for Fortune 500 firms. (118 chars — ideal: 120-160) - Viewport Meta: Present - Canonical URL: Present - Robots Meta: Present - Open Graph Tags: Present - Twitter Card: Present - Structured Data (JSON-LD): None ### Heading Structure - Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0} ## Heading Hierarchy (order as found in HTML) [["H1", "Digital experiences for the modern enterprise"], ["H2", "The world’s best companies"], ["H2", "choose to work with eight25"], ["H3", "Google"], ["H3", "Qlik"], ["H3", "Intralinks"], ["H3", "Andela"], ["H3", "Qualcomm"], ["H2", "Insights"], ["H3", "Analyzing the ‘Request a Demo’ Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging"], ["H3", "Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It"], ["H3", "Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites?"], ["H3", "Leveraging Data Analytics to Drive B2B Marketing Success"], ["H2", "Services"], ["H3", "Brand"], ["H3", "Creative"], ["H2", "Work with us"]] ## Page Content (visible text excerpt) Digital experiences for the modern enterprise We build brands and web experiences that help you build the ultimate customer journey. Let's talk The world's best companies choose to work with eight25 Privileged to work with Fortune 500 companies across the globe. Google Qlik Intralinks Andela Qualcomm Insights Analyzing the ‘Request a Demo’ Page: Unpacking Why Conversions Are Lagging Uncovering Homepage Hero Friction: Why Visitors Bounce and How to Fix It Why Is Brand Alignment Important For B2B Enterprise Websites? View all insights [...content truncated at 30,000 chars] Perform a complete website audit in two parts: **PART 1 — Analysis**: Evaluate the page across all five categories (structure, messaging, CTAs, content depth, UX). For each category, provide a score (1-10), findings grounded in specific metrics, and direct evidence. **PART 2 — Recommendations**: Based on your analysis, generate 5 prioritized recommendations. Focus on the lowest-scoring categories first. Return everything as a single structured JSON object matching the schema.

(118 chars \u2014 ideal: 120-160)", "action": "Expand the meta title to be closer to 50-60 characters and the meta description to be closer to 120-160 characters, ensuring they accurately and compellingly describe the page's content and value proposition.", "expected_impact": "Improved click-through rates from search engine results pages due to more informative and engaging meta tags."}, {"priority": 4, "category": "content", "title": "Increase External Link Diversity", "description": "The low number of external links (2) limits opportunities to cite authoritative sources, which can contribute to demonstrating E-E-A-T (Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness) and providing additional value to users.", "grounded_metric": "External Links: 2", "action": "Identify relevant, authoritative external resources (e.g., industry reports, research papers, reputable news articles) to link to from the content, particularly within the 'Insights' section or when discussing industry trends.", "expected_impact": "Enhanced E-E-A-T signals, improved credibility, and a richer user experience by providing access to further valuable information."}, {"priority": 5, "category": "seo", "title": "Utilize Deeper Heading Structures (H4-H6)", "description": "The absence of H4, H5, and H6 tags indicates a missed opportunity for more granular content organization and SEO optimization within longer content sections.", "grounded_metric": "Heading Counts: {\"h1\": 1, \"h2\": 5, \"h3\": 10, \"h4\": 0, \"h5\": 0, \"h6\": 0}", "action": "Where appropriate, break down longer H3 sections into more specific sub-topics using H4, H5, and H6 tags. This improves readability and allows for more targeted keyword integration.", "expected_impact": "Improved content scannability for users and search engines, better on-page SEO by creating more specific topic clusters within the content."}]}

structure_analysis.findings: The page has a single H1, which is good. However, there are no H4, H5, or H6 tags used, indicating a potential missed opportunity for finer content segmentation. The meta title (44 chars) is slightly short of the ideal 50-60 character range, and the meta description (118 chars) is also slightly short of the ideal 120-160 character range. Crucially, there is no structured data (JSON-LD) present, which can impact search engine understanding and rich snippet eligibility. All other technical SEO signals (viewport, canonical, robots, OG, Twitter Card) are present.
structure_analysis.evidence: Heading Counts: {"h1": 1, "h2": 5, "h3": 10, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0}. Meta Title: B2B Digital Agency for Enterprises | eight25 (44 chars). Meta Description: 118 chars. Structured Data (JSON-LD): None.

messaging_analysis.findings: The primary value proposition, 'Digital experiences for the modern enterprise,' is clear and positioned prominently. The content effectively communicates the agency's focus on B2B and enterprise clients, supported by client logos and testimonials.
messaging_analysis.evidence: H1: Digital experiences for the modern enterprise. Client logos: Google, Qlik, Intralinks, Andela, Qualcomm.

cta_analysis.findings: The page features a high number of CTAs (25), strategically placed throughout the content, including above the fold. The CTAs are action-oriented and varied.
cta_analysis.evidence: CTA Count: 25. CTAs found: 'Learn More', 'Let's talk', 'Submit', 'Read More'. Visible CTA above the fold: 'Let's talk'.

content_depth_analysis.findings: The word count of 384 is on the lower side for a comprehensive agency service page. Internal link count (49) is high, but external link count (2) is low.
content_depth_analysis.evidence: Word Count: 384. Internal Links: 49. External Links: 2.

ux_analysis.findings: The page utilizes rich visual media, including SVGs, video embeds, CSS animations, and WebGL/3D elements. All 57 images have alt text, with 55 marked as decorative. Viewport meta present.
ux_analysis.evidence: Total Images: 57. Images Missing Alt: 0. Decorative Alt: 55. Rich Visual Media: 2 SVG, video, CSS animations, WebGL/3D. Viewport: Present.

recommendations.title: Expand Content Depth for Key Service Areas
recommendations.action: Increase word count by elaborating on each service. Aim for 800-1200 words.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved topical authority and ranking potential.

recommendations.title: Implement Structured Data (JSON-LD)
recommendations.action: Implement Organization, Service, and Breadcrumb JSON-LD schema.
recommendations.expected_impact: Rich snippet eligibility and enhanced SERP visibility.

recommendations.title: Optimize Meta Title and Description Lengths
recommendations.grounded_metric: Meta title: 44 chars (ideal: 50-60). Meta description: 118 chars (ideal: 120-160).
recommendations.action: Expand meta title to 50-60 chars and description to 120-160 chars.

recommendations.title: Increase External Link Diversity
recommendations.action: Link to authoritative external resources in the Insights section.

recommendations.title: Utilize Deeper Heading Structures (H4-H6)
recommendations.action: Break longer H3 sections into H4 sub-topics where appropriate.
recommendations.expected_impact: Improved scannability and more specific topic clusters.
